skill.name: senior-architect
skill.description: Architecture guidance skill for designing scalable software systems, choosing technology stacks, reviewing trade-offs, and using lightweight analysis scripts for project reports.
skill.summary: Design scalable systems and document architecture trade-offs.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk matches, but review found these were false positives from Markdown code fences, ordinary prose, and placeholder descriptions. The only confirmed concern is low-risk filesystem output because the Python helper scripts can write JSON reports to a user-supplied path. No evidence found of network access, subprocess execution, credential collection, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: User-Controlled Report Output Path
low_findings.description: Each Python helper script writes JSON output to the path supplied through the --output option. This is a legitimate reporting feature, but it can overwrite files if a user provides an unsafe path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write calls are direct and confirmed in all three scripts. Risk is limited because the user must explicitly pass --output and no hidden write target exists.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences and documented command examples. The reviewed scripts do not invoke subprocesses or execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown blocks or ordinary documentation. No subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell execution path was found in the Python files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto and Recon Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings match words inside prose, headings, or descriptions. No hashing, encryption, credential harvesting, or host reconnaissance behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is natural language such as design descriptions and placeholder headings. No semantic evidence supports cryptographic misuse or system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Environment File Finding Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The environment file match is a setup example that copies .env.example to .env. No script reads environment files or exfiltrates environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is a documented setup command. The Python scripts import os but do not access environment variables or environment files.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Write to User-Supplied Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The --output option is passed directly to open for writing report output. Users should choose output paths carefully and avoid sensitive file locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The direct write pattern is present in each helper script. It is user-initiated and local, so it does not justify a higher risk level.

content.value_statement: Architecture decisions are hard to compare when requirements, trade-offs, and dependencies are scattered. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure designs, diagrams, and technical decisions.
actual_capabilities: Guides system architecture decisions across frontend, backend, database, mobile, and cloud technologies.
Provides reference material for architecture patterns, system design workflows, and technology choices.
Runs local Python helper scripts that validate a target path and print simple project reports.
Can produce JSON report output when a helper script is run with the --json option.
Supports architecture planning prompts for trade-off reviews, integration patterns, and dependency reviews.
limitations: The included helper scripts are placeholders and do not perform deep dependency or code analysis.
Architecture recommendations still require human validation against business goals and operational constraints.
The scripts do not generate production-ready diagrams without additional implementation work.
The --output option can overwrite files if users choose unsafe destinations.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Platform Architecture
use_cases.description: Define system boundaries, data flows, and service responsibilities before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Compare Technology Stack Options
use_cases.description: Evaluate frameworks, databases, APIs, and cloud services using structured trade-off criteria.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Convert design discussions into clear decisions, assumptions, risks, and follow-up actions.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Simple Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed application architecture. Identify the main components, risks, and the first three improvements to make.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Technology Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare three technology stack options for this product. Score each option by scalability, team fit, cost, delivery speed, and operational risk.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Integration Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an integration pattern for these services. Include data ownership, failure handling, observability, security boundaries, and migration steps.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Architecture Decision Record
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an architecture decision record for this decision. Include context, options considered, selected approach, consequences, risks, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: A team needs to decide between REST and GraphQL for a customer dashboard API.
output: A decision matrix comparing client flexibility, caching, schema governance, and operational complexity.
A recommended API approach with assumptions, risks, and adoption steps.
A list of monitoring and security checks for the chosen approach.

output_examples.input: A monolith must be split into services without disrupting product delivery.
output: A phased decomposition plan based on domain boundaries and deployment risk.
A migration sequence that preserves data ownership and rollback options.
A summary of dependencies that need explicit contracts before extraction.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead wants a project architecture report from a local repository.
output: A short status report for the analyzed target path.
A count of current findings from the helper script output.
Optional saved report output when a safe destination path is provided.

best_practices: Start with constraints, expected scale, data sensitivity, and team experience before choosing tools.
Record trade-offs and assumptions so future reviewers can understand why decisions were made.
Run helper scripts only against trusted local paths and write reports to safe output directories.
anti_patterns: Do not treat placeholder helper script output as a complete architecture assessment.
Do not choose technologies only because they are listed in the skill description.
Do not write report output over source files, secrets, or configuration files.

faq.question: Does this skill generate production-ready architecture diagrams?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture guidance and placeholder helper scripts that need implementation for real diagram generation.

faq.question: Can it analyze dependencies in a real codebase?
faq.answer: Only at a basic placeholder level. The included analyzer validates a path and emits a simple report structure.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit rates it low risk because confirmed behavior is local and user initiated.

faq.question: Why did static analysis report high-risk findings?
faq.answer: The high-risk matches came from Markdown fences, prose, and placeholder descriptions, not malicious executable behavior.

faq.question: What should users watch for when running scripts?
faq.answer: Users should choose safe target paths and avoid using --output paths that could overwrite important files.
